# Astro Library Manager Constitution

## Core Principles

### I. Local-First File Custody

Astro Library Manager MUST treat existing image files as user-owned source
material that remains on disk unless the user explicitly approves a filesystem
plan. The application MUST store metadata, relationships, decisions, manifests,
and lifecycle state without requiring raw, calibration, or processed image files
to be copied into an application-private store. Library roots MUST be modeled
separately from relative paths so moved drives and remapped roots can be
recovered without rewriting project history.

Rationale: astrophotography libraries are large, expensive to reacquire, and
often already organized across external drives and legacy folders.

### II. Reviewable Filesystem Mutation

Every move, copy, archive, delete, link, junction, symlink, or generated source
view MUST be represented as a reviewable plan before it is applied. Plan
application MUST be explicit, MUST never overwrite silently, and MUST write an
audit record for each attempted action and outcome. Destructive operations MUST
prefer archive or trash workflows over permanent deletion. Classification,
cleanup, and calibration matches MUST carry confidence levels where inference is
used.

Rationale: the product's core value is making large library changes safer, not
making them faster at the expense of reversibility and trust.

### III. PixInsight Boundary

The application MUST NOT calibrate, debayer, register, integrate, drizzle, stack,
or edit images. It MUST help users prepare PixInsight-friendly inputs, document
source usage, track outputs, and identify reviewable cleanup candidates after
final verification. Any feature that appears to replace PixInsight processing
MUST be rejected or moved to a future integration note outside v1 scope.

Rationale: keeping PixInsight/WBPP responsible for image processing preserves a
clear product boundary and prevents unsafe partial reimplementation of expert
processing workflows.

### IV. Research-Led Domain Modeling

Folder structures, naming conventions, lifecycle states, calibration reuse
rules, source view strategies, and metadata extraction rules MUST be treated as
research questions until the relevant SpecKit research decision is documented.
Where multiple plausible approaches exist, the plan MUST compare options,
record tradeoffs, and recommend a default while keeping user configuration
available for library-specific variation.

Rationale: astrophotography workflows vary by capture software, equipment,
target type, operating system, and existing library history.

### V. Portable Contracts and Durable Records

UI-to-core operations MUST be described by language-neutral contracts covering
requests, responses, errors, versioning, and long-running operation status. The
initial desktop implementation MAY use local desktop commands, but product
semantics MUST remain portable to a future local or remote service implemented
in another language. The database is the durable relationship and audit record;
generated manifests and source views are reproducible projections unless a
research decision explicitly marks an artifact as canonical.

Rationale: this preserves a migration path beyond the initial desktop shell
without binding user-facing workflows to one backend technology.

## Product Constraints

- The product MUST support existing messy libraries without forcing immediate
  migration into a single hardcoded structure.
- The product MUST support cross-platform path concerns, including Windows,
  macOS, Linux, external drives, invalid characters, case sensitivity, long
  paths, symlinks, junctions, and root remapping.
- Large-file hashing MUST be optional or lazy unless a research decision proves
  eager hashing is necessary for a specific workflow.
- The product MUST avoid following symlinks or junctions during scans unless the
  user explicitly enables that behavior for a root or operation.
- Protected categories and cleanup exclusions MUST be documented before any
  cleanup plan can be generated.

## SpecKit Workflow and Quality Gates

- Constitution checks MUST pass before Phase 0 research and be rechecked after
  Phase 1 design.
- Specifications MUST stay user- and workflow-focused; stack, schema, and API
  details belong in the implementation plan, research, data model, and
  contracts.
- Research MUST precede implementation for architecture, library organization,
  PixInsight/WBPP preparation, FITS/XISF metadata, calibration matching,
  lifecycle rules, filesystem safety, and contract strategy.
- Tasks MUST be grouped by independently testable user stories and include an
  exhaustive task dependency graph.
- No application implementation may begin until the relevant specification,
  research, plan, data model, contracts, and tasks have passed review.

## Governance

This constitution supersedes conflicting product or implementation practices.
Amendments require a documented reason, an explicit version bump, and a review
of dependent SpecKit templates and generated artifacts. Compliance is checked at
plan creation, after design, before task generation, and before implementation.

Versioning follows semantic versioning:

- MAJOR for principle removals or incompatible governance changes.
- MINOR for new principles, new required gates, or material expansions.
- PATCH for clarifications that do not change obligations.

**Version**: 1.0.0 | **Ratified**: 2026-05-02 | **Last Amended**: 2026-05-02
<!-- SPEC-KIT CONSTITUTION: END -->

## Global Instructions

For task tracking, work claiming, bd labels/metadata conventions, JSON
determinism, and multi-agent coordination, read
[beads](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/beads/.apm/context/beads.context.md).

This repo uses the Tauri MCP server to build, test, debug, and UI-drive a Tauri
v2 app. Before automating the UI, monitoring IPC, streaming logs, or writing
end-to-end tests, read [Tauri MCP context](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/mcp-tauri/.apm/context/tauri.tauri-mcp-index.context.md).

For the continuous commit/push working rhythm — atomic commits, pushing after
every meaningful step, and never stranding work in a local or disposable
worktree — read [delivery steering](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-delivery/.apm/context/delivery.delivery-index.context.md).

For branch discipline, merge-vs-PR choice, merge flags, and pre-push checks,
read [git workflow](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-git-workflow/.apm/context/git-workflow.git-workflow-index.context.md).

For communication style, epistemic defaults, and how to handle uncertainty,
tradeoffs, and brainstorming, read [pragmatic steering](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-pragmatic/.apm/context/pragmatic.pragmatic-index.context.md).

For repository layout, ownership boundaries, shared libraries, contracts, docs,
specs, tools, scripts, or product-agent assets, read
[project structure](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-project-structure/.apm/context/project-structure.project-structure-index.context.md).

## Files matching `{apps/**,**/frontend/**,**/web/**,**/*.{tsx,jsx,vue,svelte,css,scss}}`

For frontend surfaces, UI behavior, styling, browser state, server state,
accessibility, or browser verification, read
[frontend context](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-frontend/.apm/context/frontend.frontend-index.context.md).

## Files matching `{apps/**/components/**,apps/**/ui/**,libs/ui/**}`

For reusable UI components, app-local component boundaries, design-system code,
contracts, variants, or accessibility behavior, read
[frontend context](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-frontend/.apm/context/frontend.frontend-index.context.md).

## Files matching `{data/**,**/data/**,**/*.sql,**/*.ipynb}`

## Files matching `{infrastructure/**,**/*.tf,**/*.hcl,.github/workflows/**}`

For infrastructure, platform code, deployment config, CI/CD, IaC, environments,

## Files matching `{scripts/**,tools/**,justfile,Justfile,Taskfile.yml,Makefile,mise.toml,.moon/**}`
